title: add_group_member_capped uses DEFERRED not IMMEDIATE transaction
description: store.rs add_group_member_capped doc comment says BEGIN IMMEDIATE (line 886) but pool.begin() issues DEFERRED. SQLite still prevents silent cap bypass (second concurrent writer gets SQLITE_BUSY at commit time), but this error surfaces as anyhow::Err propagated as internal error 500, not the structured cap-exceeded INVALID_PARAMS response. Under concurrent GROUP_ADD near the cap, clients near the limit get opaque internal errors instead of the correct group-full message. Fix: use begin_with BEGIN IMMEDIATE so the second concurrent request fails fast at transaction start with SQLITE_BUSY, and the error path can be handled uniformly.
